name: The Recursive Astrology
license: Mixed — see each grammar's own `license` field (public-domain source texts; original synthesis CC-BY-SA-4.0)

branches.name: Primary Sources — the voices themselves, in translation
Synthesis — surveys, flagship interpretations, and readings
Readings — thematic essays, not interpretation sets

grammars.name: Alan Leo's Astrology

grammars.name: Archetypal Astrology — planetary pairs, after Tarnas
grammars.blurb: Archetypal Astrology — the planets in relationship, not sign by sign but pair by pair. Richard Tarnas's whole method began from a plain observation: every tradition that ever read the sky agreed on th…

grammars.name: The Aspects, Commented — Ptolemy · Lilly · Canonical
grammars.blurb: The five classical (Ptolemaic) aspects — conjunction, sextile, square, trine, opposition — each as an item carrying tradition-sourced commentary from two public-domain voices plus a contemporary synth…

grammars.name: Astro of All Astros — every voice, one placement
grammars.blurb: A generated meta-voice grammar aggregating every interpretation grammar in this repo into one navigable set: one item per planet, sign, and house, each carrying every voice's own reading of that place…

grammars.name: The Big Three — a reflection casting
grammars.blurb: A three-position casting that mirrors the tarot three-card spread so it works in the oracle from day one — but nothing here is drawn at random: every position resolves to an aspect of YOUR OWN chart (…

grammars.name: One Aspect — the smallest casting
grammars.blurb: The minimal casting, and the purest statement of this channel's rule: one position, filled by ANY single aspect of your own chart — a planet in its sign, a house and what stands in it, or an aspect be…

grammars.name: The Twelve Houses — a chart-wheel casting
grammars.blurb: The chart-shaped casting: the twelve houses as twelve fixed positions on the wheel — the richer target the three-position casting graduates into. Nothing is drawn at random: the positions ARE your cha…

grammars.name: Essential Dignities & Rulerships — the traditional scheme, planet by planet and sign by sign
grammars.blurb: Essential dignities and rulerships as a LENS grammar: the seven classical planets, each carrying its domicile(s), exaltation (with the traditional degree), detriment, and fall — and the twelve signs, …

grammars.name: Historiographies of Astrology
grammars.blurb: A library of the major historiographical VIEWS of astrology — the eras and stances through which humans have read meaning into the sky, each presented faithfully in its own terms and held honestly to …

grammars.name: Jyotiṣa — The Bṛhat Jātaka
grammars.blurb: The nine grahas and twelve rāśis as taught in Varāhamihira's Bṛhat Jātaka (c. 6th century CE), a foundational Sanskrit text of Hindu predictive astrology, in the 1885 English translation by N. Chidamb…

grammars.blurb: The seven classical planet-gods of Babylonian and Assyrian celestial divination — Sin, Šamaš, Ištar, Nergal, Marduk, Ninurta, and Nabû — each with a sample omen-form drawn from public-domain 19th/earl…

grammars.name: Planetary Myths — the same sky, many readings
grammars.blurb: One item per classical planet, carrying the myth that grew up around it in three unrelated traditions — Greco-Roman, Vedic (Jyotiṣa), and Babylonian/Mesopotamian — side by side, so the disagreement be…

grammars.name: Proctor's Skeptical Astrology
grammars.blurb: A critical examination of astrology based on Richard A. Proctor's 'Myths and Marvels of Astronomy' (1896). Presents traditional astrological concepts with sardonic Victorian commentary, exposing logic…

grammars.name: William Lilly's Christian Astrology
grammars.blurb: The seven classical planets and the twelve zodiac signs as William Lilly (1602–1681) describes them in Christian Astrology (1647), the most influential English-language astrology text of the Renaissan…

grammars.name: Ptolemy's Tetrabiblos
grammars.blurb: Classical Western astrology based on Claudius Ptolemy's Tetrabiblos (c. 150 CE), the foundational text that systematized Greek, Egyptian, and Babylonian astrological knowledge. Each item includes dire…

grammars.name: The Right Size
grammars.blurb: A reading on scale, awe, and the oldest shared calendar — in the spirit of How the Cards Can Work. Not whether the stars decide our fate, but what happens when we agree to relate to the visible sky as…

grammars.name: Three Doors
grammars.blurb: Reading the sky through the Trika — Parā, Parāparā, Aparā. A synthesis, offered by a non-scholar.

grammars.name: The Trika Lens — three doors into a chart

grammars.name: Western Astrology — Canonical
grammars.blurb: A canonical Western astrology grammar covering planets, signs, houses, and aspects with archetypal interpretations. A living document — contribute at github.com/PlayfulProcess/recursive.eco-schemas — …
